title: Hello World
description: The classic first program. Use the `print()` function to output text to the screen.

title: Variables
description: Variables are containers for storing data values. You don't need to declare a type, just assign a value.

title: Data Types
description: Python has various data types like integers, floats, strings, and booleans. You can use `type()` to check.
codeSnippet:
x = 10
y = 3.14
is_python_fun = True
print(type(x), type(y), type(is_python_fun))

title: Basic Math
description: You can perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division easily.
codeSnippet:
a = 15
b = 4
print(a + b)
print(a / b)

title: Strings
description: Strings are sequences of characters. You can combine them with the `+` operator or use f-strings for formatting.
codeSnippet:
first = "Python"
last = "Learning"
result = first + " " + last
print(result)
print(f"Hello {first}!")

title: Lists
description: Lists are ordered collections of items. You can access items by index starting from 0.

title: Loops - For
description: For loops let you iterate over sequences. Perfect for repeating code multiple times.

title: Conditionals
description: Use if, elif, and else to make decisions in your code based on conditions.

title: Functions
description: Functions are reusable blocks of code. Define with `def` and call them by name.
codeSnippet:
def greet(name):
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

result = greet("Alice")
print(result)

title: Dictionaries
description: Dictionaries store data in key-value pairs. Access values using their keys.

title: List Comprehension
description: Create lists in a concise way using list comprehension. It's a powerful Python feature.
codeSnippet:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = [x**2 for x in numbers]
print(squares)
evens = [x for x in numbers if x % 2 == 0]
print(evens)

title: Lambda Functions
description: Lambda functions are small anonymous functions. Use them for quick, simple operations.
codeSnippet:
add = lambda x, y: x + y
result = add(5, 3)
print(result)

numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squared = map(lambda x: x**2, numbers)
print(list(squared))

title: Error Handling
description: Use try and except to handle errors gracefully instead of crashing.

title: Slicing
description: Slicing lets you extract parts of sequences using [start:end:step] notation.
codeSnippet:
text = "Python"
print(text[0:3])
print(text[2:])
print(text[::-1])

nums = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
print(nums[1:4])
print(nums[::2])

title: While Loops
description: While loops continue until a condition becomes false. Be careful of infinite loops!
codeSnippet:
count = 0
while count < 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1
print("Done!")

title: Nested Loops
description: Loops inside loops let you work with 2D data and complex iterations.

title: String Methods
description: Strings have built-in methods like upper(), lower(), split(), and replace().
codeSnippet:
text = "Python is Fun"
print(text.upper())
print(text.lower())
print(text.split())
print(text.replace("Fun", "Awesome"))
output:
PYTHON IS FUN
python is fun
['Python', 'is', 'Fun']
Python is Awesome

title: File Handling
description: Read and write files using Python. Always close files or use 'with' statement.
codeSnippet:
with open("example.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello, World!")

with open("example.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
    print(content)
